from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple phrase mapping for better readability; fall back to a generic pattern.
    phrase_map = {
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        # more natural wording for position
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o.lower()}" if o[0].lower() not in "aeiou" else f"{s} plays as an {o.lower()}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        # current club is a common predicate
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was picked at {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        # Revised phrasing for coach to place person first
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        # more natural phrasing for city relationship
        "city": lambda s, o: f"The home of {s} is the city of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{o} are an ethnic group in {s}",
        # Revised language phrasing, strip trailing ' language' if present
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language spoken in {s} is {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"The general manager of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        # Revised demonym phrasing, put demonym first
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a demonym of {s}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"The stadium of {s} is {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"The {s} are the university team for {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The team with most championships in {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"The UTC offset of {s} is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"The time zone of {s} is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"The total area of {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
    }

    def make_phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
        # Clean object string: strip whitespace and surrounding quotes
        obj = t.object.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        func = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if func:
            return func(t.subject, obj)
        # generic fallback
        return f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}"

    # Group triples by (subject, predicate) to allow aggregation of objects
    groups: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        obj = t.object.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        groups.setdefault(key, []).append(obj)

    def join_objects(objs: list[str]) -> str:
        # Helper to join a list with commas and 'and' before the last item
        if not objs:
            return ""
        if len(objs) == 1:
            return objs[0]
        if len(objs) == 2:
            return f"{objs[0]} and {objs[1]}"
        return ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"

    agg_phrases: list[str] = []
    for (subj, pred), objs in groups.items():
        # Clean objects (strip quotes already done)
        if len(objs) == 1:
            # Single object – use normal phrasing
            agg_phrases.append(make_phrase(Triple(subj, pred, objs[0])))
        else:
            # Multiple objects – create an aggregated phrase for specific predicates
            if pred == "club":
                # prepend 'the' if not already present for nicer style
                objs = [o if o.lower().startswith("the ") else f"the {o}" for o in objs]
                agg_phrases.append(f"{subj}'s clubs are {join_objects(objs)}")
            elif pred == "formerTeam":
                agg_phrases.append(f"{subj} formerly played for {join_objects(objs)}")
            elif pred == "currentclub":
                agg_phrases.append(f"{subj} currently plays for {join_objects(objs)}")
            else:
                # generic aggregation
                agg_phrases.append(f"{subj} {pred}s are {join_objects(objs)}")

    # Combine all aggregated phrases into one sentence
    if not agg_phrases:
        return ""
    if len(agg_phrases) == 1:
        sentence = agg_phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(agg_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + agg_phrases[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
